questions.prompt: Why can't you just outbid your way to better Meta Ads performance?

choices.text: Auctions are won on total value, roughly bid × estimated action rate × ad quality. EAR is the ML-predicted probability the user takes your optimized action; ad quality is composite signals from creative and post-click experience. A 2x bid cannot compensate for a 5x worse EAR. Better creative + better targeting + better post-click experience raise EAR and quality; that's the lever. The system rewards predicted relevance, not just dollars.

questions.explanation: Total value = bid × EAR × quality. EAR and quality are the levers; bid alone can't overcome them.

questions.prompt: Optimizing for 'link clicks' produces clicks but no purchases. Why?

choices.text: EAR predicts the optimization event you specified. If you optimize for link clicks, the ML finds users likely to click — not users likely to buy. Different audiences, different intent, different conversion behavior. The system did exactly what you asked for. Fix: optimize for purchases (or whatever bottom-funnel event matters) with proper conversion tracking (pixel + Conversions API). Volume of conversion events affects ML quality — if too few, optimize for a higher-funnel proxy like 'add to cart' while you build conversion volume.

questions.explanation: Optimize for the event you actually want. EAR finds users likely to take that specific action.
